text: The surgeon mobilizes the short gastric vessels from the mid portion of the splenic hilum to the gastroesophageal junction using a bipolar device and hook. The left pillar of the crura is dissected, and the fat pad is resected to fully expose the fundus.

The surgeon identifies the mostly intraluminal lesion in the posterior aspect of the stomach, approximately three centimeters from the gastroesophageal junction. An intraoperative endoscopy confirms the lesion's location. A 52 French bougie is inserted, and an anterior gastrotomy is performed about four centimeters from the gastroesophageal junction.

The surgeon lifts the tumor from the posterior aspect of the gastric wall. Two Endo GIA staplers with purple loads are fired across the tumor, ensuring a good margin of mucosa. The lesion is then placed in an endopouch for extraction and sent for histopathological analysis.
The frozen section analysis reveals a negative margin with no infiltration at the suture line. The surgeon then over-sews the suture line using 3-0 PDS sutures.

The surgeon closes the anterior gastrotomy using running 3-0 VLOG sutures and interrupted 3-0 PDS sutures. An intraoperative endoscopy confirms the absence of intragastric bleeding or leaks, and shows good passage through the gastroesophageal junction.
